title: FOIA Documents ‘Scrubbed’ From Intelligence Agency Website
summary: Last year, the Office of the Director of National Intelligence removed all documents from its FOIA reading room. Emails say a Biden-era FOIA release caused a “fuss.”

title: Standard Chartered Said to Arrange $2.2 Billion Tanzania Rail Loan
summary: Standard Chartered Plc has arranged a $2.2 billion loan with export credit agencies and development finance institutions for Tanzania’s landmark rail project, according to Turkish contractor Yapi Merkezi Insaat VE Sanayi AS.

title: Cava’s Only Bearish Analyst Says 123% Rally Has Gone Too Far
summary: A rally that’s more than doubled shares of Mediterranean fast-casual chain Cava Group Inc. in five months has gone too far, according to its new lone bear on Wall Street.

summary: Source: Bloomberg, 0:00

title: Votes and Verdicts: Treyz on Trump, Iran, Fed, Midterms, Tariffs
summary: What role will Congress play in the future of the Iran conflict? How will the outcome of the US midterm elections affect companies like Apple, Bank of America and Amazon.com? Will President Donald Trump spend the last years of his presidency focused on tariffs? These questions and more were discussed on our latest episode of Votes and Verdicts, where Veda Partners co-founder and director of economic policy Henrietta Treyz joined us. title: Fuel Turmoil Creates an Opening for an African Billionaire
summary: Aliko Dangote’s oil refinery has been inundated with requests since the war in Iran started.

title: Brazil Moves to Ban Prediction Markets on Elections, Sports
summary: Brazil moved to ban prediction markets tied to elections and sporting events, tightening rules on a booming practice that’s drawing scrutiny across the world.

title: Stocks Set for Record High on US-Iran Talk Hopes: Markets Wrap
summary: Hopes that the US and Iran will be back to the negotiating table drove stocks toward all-time highs, with the market also rising after a blowout forecast from Intel Corp. and the closing of a probe into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

title: Energy Merchant Six One Overtakes Trafigura in US Gas Trading
summary: Six One Commodities LLC has more than tripled its physical US natural gas trading activity, catapulting the firm above heavyweight commodity houses Trafigura Trading, Freepoint Commodities and Hartree Partners.

title: Milei Incentives Set to Boost $12 Billion Vaca Muerta Oil Field
summary: President Javier Milei’s marquee investment incentive package is set to bring forward development of a flagship oil field in Argentina’s booming Vaca Muerta shale patch.

title: Baker Hughes | Outlook Despite Middle East Disruption
summary: Baker Hughes beats on earnings, sees outlook hold despite Mideast disruption. Lorenzo Simonelli, president and CEO of Baker Hughes, says the “outlook for the business fundamentals remains unchanged,” excluding the ongoing impacts in the Middle East. He spoke with Dani Burger and Matt Miller on Open Interest. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Chick-fil-A Sells $650 Million of Investment-Grade Private Debt
summary: Fast-food chain Chick-fil-A Inc. sold $650 million of bonds in a private placement, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

title: Treasuries Gain as DOJ Drops Fed Probe, Opening Path for Warsh
summary: Treasuries edged higher after the Justice Department dropped its investigation into the Federal Reserve, potentially opening a path for President Donald Trump’s pick Kevin Warsh to become Fed chair and push for interest-rate cuts.

title: The Real Problem with Private Credit
summary: Credit that turns out to behave a lot like equity.

title: Amazon-Backed X-Energy Climbs 31% After $1.02 Billion US IPO
summary: X-Energy Inc. shares opened 31% above their IPO price, after the nuclear energy firm raised $1.02 billion in an upsized US initial public offering.

title: Bessent Casts Potential New Swap Lines as Boost to Dollar’s Role
summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent talked up the idea of extending currency swap lines to more countries as a way to strengthen “dollar dominance” in a social media post Friday as speculation continues over why rich countries such as the United Arab Emirates would be interested in the arrangement.

title: Oil Falls on Report US Delegation to Travel For Iran Talks
summary: Oil fell to intraday lows amid fresh signs that elusive peace talk plans between the US and Iran may materialize after all, stoking hopes of a resumption to flows though the vital Strait of Hormuz.

title: European Stocks Fall as Investors Await Word of Iran Peace Talks
summary: European stocks fell as investors tracked developments around potential US-Iran peace talks amid a continued standoff in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: CVC’s Maticmind Tries to Calm Bondholders as Founder Probed
summary: Maticmind SpA said all its published financial information is “complete and correct” as it sought to reassure investors after a fraud probe into the software firm’s founder sent its bonds sliding.

title: Nasdaq 100 Heads for Record After Intel’s Blockbuster Guidance
summary: The Nasdaq 100 Index was on track for a fresh all-time closing high on Friday as a blockbuster sales forecast from Intel Corp. fueled a rally in technology stocks. Meanwhile, traders optimism about the Middle East increased as Iran’s foreign minister said he was headed to Pakistan.

title: Crypto Faithful Wins Memecoin Lunch with Trump for $500
summary: Last year, Morten Christensen spent about $1,200 to attend a dinner with President Donald Trump reserved for the top holders of the president’s memecoin. This year, he’s going to a similar event for just around $500.

title: Gold Gains on Iran Talks Optimism With Fed Outlook in Focus
summary: Gold advanced on optimism that the US and Iran are moving toward talks after days of deadlock, with traders also asessing the Federal Reserve’s interest rate path after the Justice Department dropped a probe of Chair Jerome Powell.

title: Euro Zone Price Spike Seen Surging Higher in Second Month of War
summary: Euro-zone inflation probably jumped in April to the highest in 2 1/2 years as fallout from the Iran war squeezed economies across the currency area.

title: Azerbaijan Sold Gold Worth $3 Billion in First Ever Drawdown
summary: Azerbaijan’s State Oil Fund sold about 22 tons of gold in the first quarter of the year, after a record-breaking rally pushed the sovereign wealth fund’s allocation of the metal to its maximum threshold.

title: DOJ Drops Powell Probe, Smoothing Path for Warsh to Fed
summary: The Justice Department is ending a controversial investigation into building-renovation cost overruns by the Federal Reserve, potentially clearing a path to confirmation for Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s pick to be the next Fed leader. Bloomberg's Michael McKee breaks the news on "Bloomberg Intelligence." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ‘The Jig Is Up’: 70% Crash Marks Quick End to Avis Short Squeeze
summary: The speculative mania around Avis Budget Group Inc. erupted quickly. It collapsed even faster.

title: Pakistan Expects to Host Another Round of US-Iran Talks
summary: Pakistani officials expect to host another round of peace talks between Iran and the US. Iran's top diplomat is going there, but it's not clear if a US delegation is as well. Retired US Army Colonel Wayne Sanders, a Bloomberg Intelligence analyst, is on Bloomberg Open Interest.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Pakistan Buys LNG Supply From Spot Market to Ease Energy Crunch
summary: Pakistan purchased a liquefied natural gas shipment from the expensive spot market for the first time in over two years to ease a shortage caused by the conflict in the Middle East.

title: Bloomberg Surveillance 4/24/2026
summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fuel Shortages Reappear Across Bolivia, Rattling New US Ally
summary: Bolivia is running out of fuel again, challenging a new government that had vowed to fix the economy after two decades of socialist rule.

title: Intel Plans Bond Sale for Repurchase of Plant Stake From Apollo
summary: Intel Corp. is readying a potential bond sale to finance the repurchase of half of a plant in Ireland that it had previously sold to Apollo Global Management Inc..

title: Hut 8 Readies High-Grade Bond Sale for Google-Backed Data Center
summary: Hut 8 Corp. is considering tapping the investment-grade debt market to fund construction of a data center linked to Alphabet Inc.’s Google, adding to a wave of borrowing driven by the artificial intelligence boom.

title: Iran ‘Will Always Believe’ They Control Hormuz
summary:
"We all know they can close it again."

Iran now firmly believes it controls the Strait of Hormuz, former Deputy US Secretary of State Wendy Sherman says. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US, EU Reach Critical Minerals Deal to Weaken China’s Grip
summary: The US and European Union on Friday reached an agreement to coordinate on securing critical minerals and bolstering supply chains, in a move meant to reduce reliance on China for rare earths and permanent magnets.

title: SNB Chief Economist Lenz to Retire, With Brown Set to Take Over
summary: Swiss National Bank Chief Economist Carlos Lenz is set to retire at the end of the year and will be succeeded by Martin Brown, the current director of the central bank’s academic research institution.

title: One by One, Europe’s Safest Government Bonds Fall From Grace
summary: The traditional hierarchy of debt in Europe is facing its latest shake-up as worsening public finances in Belgium threaten to turn some of the region’s once-safest bonds into a risky bet.
